text:
Once upon a time in a small town hidden behind old oaks, there lived an old wise owl. The owl was revered for knowing everything about everyone. One rainy afternoon, a young girl named Lily stumbled into the woods looking lost.

"Owl!" she gasped. The owl hooted softly. Lily hadn't heard anything. Just listened. Just listened some more.

"I'm here," she said. "Thank you."

The owl merely hooted again.

That night, the owl saw something in Lily's eyes. Concern. Something she cared about. Over weeks, Lily became one of the owl's favorites. She'd listen to stories. Tell secrets. Just talk.

And then one winter night, a big snow fell. So hard, the trees cracked. Lily didn't come out. Owls are nocturnal. They flew worried.

Then dawn broke. No Lily. Owl flew off.

Days passed. Then owl saw flickering. Went closer. Sunlight on snow.
